lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Did my father strike my gentleman for chiding of his fool?

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Yes, madam.

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: By day and night he wrongs me; every hour

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: He flashes into one gross crime or other,

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: That sets us all at odds: I’ll not endure it:

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: His knights grow riotous, and himself upbraids us

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: On every trifle. When he returns from hunting,

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: I will not speak with him; say I am sick:

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: If you come slack of former services,

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: You shall do well; the fault of it I’ll answer.

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: He’s coming, madam; I hear him. Horns within.

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Put on what weary negligence you please,

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: You and your fellows; I’ll have it come to question:

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: If he dislike it, let him to our sister,

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Whose mind and mine, I know, in that are one,

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Not to be over-ruled. Idle old man,

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: That still would manage those authorities

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: That he hath given away! Now, by my life,

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Old fools are babes again; and must be used

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: With cheques as flatteries—when they are seen abused.

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: Remember what I tell you.

lines.speaker: OSWALD
lines.text: Well, madam.

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: And let his knights have colder looks among you;

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: What grows of it, no matter; advise your fellows so:

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: I would breed from hence occasions, and I shall,

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: That I may speak: I’ll write straight to my sister,

lines.speaker: GONERIL
lines.text: To hold my very course. Prepare for dinner.

stage_directions.text: Enter Goneril, and Oswald, her steward.

glosses.definition: fall short of the service we used to give
doting old man — Goneril's cold formula for her father
still wants to wield the powers
proverb — the old become children a second time

annotations.title: Goneril's strategy — speed, provocation, documented grievance
annotations.body: Goneril's brief twenty-seven-line scene with Oswald is a compressed study in practical politics. She orders the household to slack service to Lear, encourages grievances, tells Oswald she 'would breed from hence occasions' — manufactured provocations. The plan is to make Lear angry enough that his anger itself will provide cover for the retinue-reduction she has already decided to impose. This is Shakespeare's quickest demonstration of how the sisters operate: by creating the appearance of Lear's instability so that the punishment they intend can be framed as response rather than premeditation. Goneril anticipates modern PR.
sources: Marilyn French, Shakespeare's Division of Experience (1981)

options: To patiently care for her father
To instruct her household to provoke Lear — manufacturing grievances so that her planned retinue-reduction can appear to respond to his bad behavior rather than precede it
